mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending consumer spending contraction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate profit forecasts and lifting equity valuations across cyclical sectors over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any rolling 3-year period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its long-term trend while capacity utilization remains elevated indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO sustains values above 100 for consecutive quarters, or the signal exhibits no directional skew in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive equity returns over a three-month investment horizon regardless of valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth decouples from corporate earnings growth such that positive GDP quarters consistently produce negative equity returns, breaking the historical correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy prematurely, raising real interest rates and compressing equity risk premiums, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally during periods of accelerating inflation, or the forward return distribution shows no negative skew when the inflation threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market where payroll growth accelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines confirms structural economic strength, reducing recession risk and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict equity direction better than a random coin flip over a 10-year backtest, or the interaction coefficient turns negative during historical labor shortages.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential investment signals a collapse in household balance sheets and credit availability, which transmits to broader economic activity and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Residential investment declines do not correlate with subsequent equity drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive despite sustained monthly drops in the series.
